titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to David F. Phillips Papers
titlestmt.author: by Tina Evans

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid is written in English.

Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 15, 2024 at 04:19

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 45 (student records): Restricted. Not to be consulted before January 1, 2050.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this collection roughly maintain order found at the time of acquisition.

bioghist.head: David F. Phillips (1944 - 2020)
bioghist.p: David F. Phillips was an American scholar, heraldist, lawyer, librarian, and writer. Born in Delaware in 1944, Phillips attended Columbia University, University of Pennsylvania Law School, and the Columbia University School of Library Service. In addition to law and librarianship, Phillips traveled and studied extensively. Among his many associates, Phillips became close friends with Rosemary Woodruff Leary and Timothy Leary. He established radbash.com as an online home for his writings and scholarship.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: David F. Phillips Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The David F. Phillips papers contain correspondence, writings, artwork, photographs, printed material, content from Radbash.com, and other papers by or relating to David F. Phillips. When possible, description created by Phillips's estate has been repurposed.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The David F. Phillips Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: DFP's personal flag and coat of arms
DFP's drawings for his flag and variations
Drawings by Maitreya Bowen for heraldic tile based on DFP coat of arms
Autobiography: overview of chapters & front matter
Series: memoir portrait of D. F. P.
Relics: 1940's birth documents, letters, announcements, horoscope
Relics 1940s D. F. P. baby book
Relics: 1940s including photographs and birthday cards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a drawing by Phillips on the last page.

did.unittitle: Relics 1960s Conscientious objector draft claim text
Relics 1970s: Legal brief by DFP-Air pirates
Relics 1977-1982 Farella Braun & Martel librarian memos and notes
Relics 1980s San Francisco State U. M. A. program in Humanities
List of friends by period when they met
Annette Barbasch Sophie Barbasch Kevin Matthews
Book collecting: dealer lists and strategies-various cities
Annoted [Annotated] Ulysses: DP's notes in margin from William York Tindall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes political, personal, elegaic, religious, and literary essays by Phillips.

did.unittitle: Other writing: additional writing not on Radbash.com
D. F. P. letters to Arianna
Snake stories, animal stories, poems for Arianna
Published heraldic articles by D. F. P.
DFP's work for Flag Heritage Series
Book proposal: Heraldry for the senses
Heraldic drawing by DFP for heraldic course, England
2 Memos re: applying a heraldic sensibility to a logo design
Correspondence: William Beaver The Heraldic Craftsman
José Raphael; Roldán to Whitney Smith
Flag Heritage Foundation: Wanamaker Flag Collection
Visit to Briscoe Center Report to Flag Heritage Foundation
Flag Heritage Foundation DFP's text for website
Timothy Leary and Rosemary Woodruff Leary
Photograph of Rosemary Woodruff Leary with DFP & Bernie Segal
Press reports Rosemary Woodruff Leary gains freedom
Psychedelic refugee by Rosemary Woodruff Leary
Memoir by Jordan of Rosemary Woodruff Leary & her memorial service, April 2002
Letter from Timothy Leary to Rosemary Woodruff Leary
Memo re Joel Solkoff and Joanna Harcourt-Smith
Timothy Leary: Seven levels of neurological drugs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains unsorted papers grouped by Phillips.
